PROMPT: I'm planning a multi-stop research trip across three iconic US national parks and need a comprehensive comparison. Please do the following: First, find all national parks in California (CA), Utah (UT), and Wyoming (WY) — I need to know the full list so I can pick representative parks. From that list, identify the park codes for: Yosemite National Park, Zion National Park, and Yellowstone National Park. Then, for each of those three parks, retrieve their detailed information (entrance fees, description, coordinates) and current alerts. Next, use the coordinates of each park's first listed entrance to geocode the address and find the nearest Google Maps place details — specifically look up 'visitor center' near each park's coordinates and get the place_id for the top result, then retrieve the place details for each. After that, using the coordinates you extracted from each park's details, get the current weather at each of the three parks. Finally, calculate the driving distance matrix from Yosemite National Park headquarters (37.8651,-119.5383) to both Zion National Park headquarters (37.2982,-113.0263) and Yellowstone National Park headquarters (44.4280,-110.5885), and also from Zion to Yellowstone. Compile a final summary stating: (1) the number of active alerts at each of the three parks, (2) the current temperature (°F) at each park, (3) the driving distance in km between each pair of parks (Yosemite↔Zion, Yosemite↔Yellowstone, Zion↔Yellowstone), and (4) the name and formatted address of the visitor center place found nearest to Yellowstone's coordinates.
